summary: An external IP from Moscow, Russia (Selectel AS49505), flagged as suspicious with 77 brute-force reports in the past 90 days, made 7 failed SSH login attempts against the 'webadmin' account on web-01 before achieving a successful login — all within 96 seconds. The max_auth_exceeded event confirms automated tooling was used. recommended_actions: IMMEDIATE: Terminate any active SSH sessions on web-01 originating from 45.38.151.9 using 'who', 'w', or 'ss -tp' and kill the session with 'pkill -u webadmin' or by closing the PTY.

Lock the 'webadmin' account on web-01 ('passwd -l webadmin') and rotate its SSH credentials — password and any authorized keys — treating all existing credentials as compromised.

Conduct forensic triage on web-01: check /var/log/auth.log, bash history (~webadmin/.bash_history), /tmp and /var/tmp for dropped files, new cron jobs, new user accounts, and any SUID binaries created after 08:18 UTC.
Search across all other hosts for SSH connections from 45.38.151.9 and for logins using the 'webadmin' account to determine if lateral movement occurred.
Escalate to Incident Response and preserve a snapshot/memory image of web-01 before any remediation steps that would destroy evidence.

Review and harden SSH configuration: enforce key-based authentication only, disable password auth in sshd_config, and implement fail2ban or equivalent rate-limiting to prevent future brute-force success.
